title: Global Multi-Asset Market Overview: Technology Rally Drives Risk-On Sentiment Amid Geopolitical Relief
intro: Global markets closed at record highs on May 7, 2026, driven by optimism surrounding US-Iran ceasefire negotiations and exceptional AI infrastructure earnings results. The technology sector led a broad-based rally, while geopolitical risk premiums compressed across asset classes, creating favorable conditions for risk assets.

sections.heading: US Equity Markets: Technology Leadership Fuels Record Highs
sections.content: US equity indices delivered robust gains, with the S&P 500 advancing +1.46% to close at record levels, while the Nasdaq 100 surged +2.08% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained +1.24%. The rally was underpinned by exceptional earnings from semiconductor and AI infrastructure companies. Advanced Micro Devices led the charge with gains exceeding +17% after reporting Q1 revenue of $10.25 billion (consensus: $9.89 billion) and guiding Q2 revenue to $10.90-11.50 billion range (well above consensus $10.52 billion). Super Micro Computer soared +24% following Q4 net sales guidance of $11.0-12.5 billion (midpoint significantly above consensus $11.16 billion). The semiconductor complex saw broad-based strength, with ARM Holdings gaining +12%, Lam Research up +7%, ASML advancing +6%, and Nvidia climbing +5%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) and E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) tracked spot performance, gaining +1.45% and +2.09% respectively.

sections.heading: Currency Markets: USD Strength and JPY Intervention Risks
sections.content: The US Dollar maintained its dominance across G10 currencies, posting an average gain of +0.12% intraday. Currency strength ranking from strongest to weakest showed: USD (+0.12%) > AUD (+0.07%) > NZD (+0.07%) > JPY (+0.07%) > GBP (+0.01%) > EUR (unchanged) > CHF (-0.12%). USD/JPY advanced to 156.3890, approaching the critical 157.00 intervention zone amid intensifying verbal warnings from the Bank of Japan. EUR/USD consolidated at 1.1754 below 1.18 resistance, pressured by dovish ECB rhetoric and weak German manufacturing data. GBP/USD traded sideways at 1.3596 ahead of BoE Governor Bailey's speech. AUD/USD benefited from commodity strength and China reopening optimism, reaching 0.7250. Positioning flows revealed high USD demand from Asian corporates for month-end hedging, while JPY shorts remained crowded despite intervention warnings, with stop-losses concentrated above 157.00.

sections.heading: Global Asset Performance and Regional Dynamics
sections.content: Asian markets extended the global risk-on sentiment, with the Nikkei 225 delivering an impressive +5.95% surge to 63,054, driven by continued yen weakness boosting export competitiveness and strong corporate earnings growth. European indices participated in the rally, with CAC 40 gaining +2.94% to 8,299 on luxury and industrial sector strength, while FTSE 100 advanced +2.15% to 10,439 led by energy sector outperformance. Commodity markets showed mixed performance: Gold consolidated at $4,708.60 (+0.30%) above $4,700 support with geopolitical premiums intact, WTI crude tested $96 resistance at $95.66 (+0.61%) on supply disruption narratives, Silver outperformed with +1.41% gains to $78.39 on industrial demand, while Natural Gas declined -0.29% to $2.722 on mild weather forecasts. Cryptocurrency markets showed profit-taking pressure, with Bitcoin consolidating at $81,062 (-0.44%) below $82K resistance and Ethereum weakening to $2,329.59 (-0.90%) as the ETH/BTC ratio declined.

sections.heading: Strategic Trading Opportunities and Risk Assessment
sections.content: Current market dynamics present several high-probability directional opportunities. Long positioning favors USD/JPY targeting 157.00 on momentum continuation (probability: 75%), Nikkei futures on sustained yen weakness (probability: 70%), WTI crude above $95.50 on supply deficit narratives (probability: 65%), and Silver on industrial demand tailwinds (probability: 60%). Short opportunities include EUR/USD below 1.1750 targeting 1.1700 (probability: 65%), Natural Gas on seasonal headwinds (probability: 70%), Bitcoin below $80,500 for retesting $79K support (probability: 55%), and European banking sector on yield curve concerns (probability: 60%). Key catalysts include US Initial Jobless Claims (13:30 CET), German Industrial Production, ECB President Lagarde's speech (15:00 CET), and continued monitoring of 10-year Treasury yields approaching 4.85% resistance.

quote: The Nikkei 225's remarkable +5.95% surge to 63,054 exemplifies how currency dynamics can amplify equity momentum in today's interconnected markets.
conclusion: The current market environment reflects a confluence of reduced geopolitical risk premiums and robust technology sector fundamentals. While USD strength and potential BoJ intervention at 157.00 present near-term tactical risks, the underlying momentum in AI infrastructure investments and global risk appetite suggests continued upside potential. Investors should monitor US Treasury yield dynamics closely, as a break above 4.85% on the 10-year could shift the risk-reward calculus across asset classes.
excerpt: Global markets hit records on US-Iran ceasefire optimism and stellar AI earnings results.
